title: senecio helenitis subsp. candidus
intro: Senecio helenitis subsp. candidus, also known as White Jacob's Ladder, is a perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant's health benefits include relieving congestion, reducing fever, and soothing irritated throats and respiratory tracts, making it a valuable remedy for various respiratory ailments. In herbal preparations, White Jacob's Ladder is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, syrups, or added to topical creams and salves for skin issues.
